Examples.Title: Get Batch Magic Prompt Job Results if available.
Examples.Description: Gets the results of a batch magic prompt job if available.
Examples.Code:
### Get Batch Magic Prompt Job Results if available.
# @name get_maybe_batch_results
GET {{host}}/internal/batch/get-maybe-batch-results/{{job_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Batch magic prompt job results if available.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized for batch magic prompt job results request.
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.

Examples.Title: Initiate Batch Magic Prompt Evalution
Examples.Description:
Runs automated evaluation of multiple LLM models and system prompts for magic prompt generation. 
Generates images using large batches of user prompt inputs. Internal use only (feature flagged).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Create a new dataset
Examples.Description: Creates a new dataset
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: List datasets
Examples.Description: Lists all datasets for the authenticated user
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Train a model from a dataset
Examples.Description: Start training a custom model from a dataset. The dataset must contain at least 15 images. Returns the model ID and training status.
Examples.Code:
### Train a model from a dataset
# @name train_dataset_model
POST {{host}}/datasets/{{dataset_id}}/train_model
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model_name": "my-custom-model",
  "lora_rank": 64,
  "num_chips": 32,
  "ema": 0.999,
  "base_model_version": "V_3_1",
  "training_steps": 1000,
  "learning_rate": 0.0001
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Training started successfully
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized
# 404
# Description: Dataset not found

Examples.Title: Upload assets to a dataset
Examples.Description: Upload image assets to a dataset for model training. Accepts individual images (JPEG, PNG, WebP), optional .txt caption sidecar files, and/or ZIP archives containing images and captions. A dataset can contain up to 100 images.
Examples.Code:
### Upload assets to a dataset
# @name upload_dataset_assets
POST {{host}}/datasets/{{dataset_id}}/upload_assets
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Upload summary
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized
# 404
# Description: Dataset not found

Examples.Title: Edit (legacy)
Examples.Description:
Edit a given image synchronously using the provided mask. The mask indicates which part of the image
should be edited, while the prompt and chosen style type can further guide the edit.

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Edit (legacy)
# @name post_edit_image
POST {{host}}/edit
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Image edits generated successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 422
# Description: Prompt or Initial Image failed the safety checks.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Edit with Ideogram V2
Examples.Description:
Edit one or more images using a text prompt. Provide images via file upload
or Ideogram image URLs, and describe the desired edit in your prompt.

Unlike inpainting (/v1/ideogram-v3/inpaint), no mask is required. The model interprets the
prompt to determine what to change.

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Edit with Ideogram V2
# @name post_edit_image_v2_v3
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/instructional-edit
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Image edits generated successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 402
# Description: Insufficient credits or quota.
# 422
# Description: Prompt or provided image failed safety checks.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.
# 503
# Description: Image generation service temporarily unavailable.

Examples.Title: Generate a design from a text prompt with text detection and correction
Examples.Description:
Generates a design synchronously from a text prompt using the Ideogram 3.0 model, then detects and corrects text layers within the generated image.

The response includes the generated image URL along with detected text layers (with font, size, color, position) and image asset layers.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Generate a design from a text prompt with text detection and correction
# @name post_generate_design_v3
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/generate-design
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "prompt": "A birthday card saying \u0027Happy Birthday\u0027",
  "seed": 12345,
  "resolution": "1280x800",
  "aspect_ratio": "1x3",
  "rendering_speed": "DEFAULT",
  "magic_prompt": "ON",
  "negative_prompt": "brush strokes, painting",
  "num_images": 1,
  "color_palette": {
    "name": "PASTEL"
  },
  "style_codes": [
    "AAFF5733",
    "0133FF57",
    "DE3357FF"
  ],
  "style_type": "GENERAL",
  "style_preset": "BRIGHT_ART",
  "custom_model_uri": "model/my-custom-model/version/1",
  "font_file_h1": "",
  "font_name_h1": "Ubuntu-Bold.ttf",
  "font_file_h2": "",
  "font_name_h2": "string",
  "font_file_body": "",
  "font_name_body": "string",
  "font_file_small": "",
  "font_name_small": "string",
  "style_reference_images": [
    ""
  ],
  "reference_asset_images": [
    ""
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Design(s) generated successfully with text correction.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 422
# Description: Prompt failed the safety check.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Generate (legacy)
Examples.Description:
Generates images synchronously based on a given prompt and optional parameters.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

    ""
  ],

    ""
  ],

}

Examples.Title: Generate with Ideogram 3.0 (Transparent Background)
Examples.Description:
Generates images with transparent background synchronously based on a given prompt and optional parameters using
the Ideogram 3.0 model. Images will be generated using maximum supported resolution at the specified aspect ratio
to allow best results with upscaler. The selected resolution is written to the response, not the upscaled final
resolution.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Generate with Ideogram 3.0 (Transparent Background)
# @name post_generate_image_v3_transparent
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/generate-transparent
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "prompt": "A photo of a cat",
  "seed": 12345,
  "upscale_factor": "X2",
  "aspect_ratio": "1x3",
  "rendering_speed": "DEFAULT",
  "magic_prompt": "ON",
  "negative_prompt": "brush strokes, painting",
  "num_images": 1,
  "custom_model_uri": "model/my-custom-model/version/1"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Image(s) generated successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 422
# Description: Prompt failed the safety check.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Layerize an existing flat graphic image with text detection and correction
Examples.Description:
Processes an existing flat graphic image to detect and correct text layers without generating a new image from scratch.

The uploaded image is analyzed for text content, which is then removed and returned as structured text layers with font matching, sizing, and color information.

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP (max size 10MB).

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Layerize an existing flat graphic image with text detection and correction
# @name post_layerize_design_v3
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/layerize-design
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Design layerized successfully with text correction.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Layerize Text
Examples.Description:
Analyzes an image to detect text regions, then returns each detected text block with its position, content, font information, and styling.

The response includes a text-erased base image (background with all text removed) and a flat list of detected text blocks.

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP (max size 10MB).

Image links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Layerize Text
# @name post_layerize_text_v3
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/layerize-text
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Text layers detected and extracted successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized.
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Replace Background with Ideogram 3.0
Examples.Description:
Replace the background of a given image synchronously using a prompt with Ideogram 3.0. The foreground subject
will be identified and kept, while the background is replaced based on the prompt and chosen style.
Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP.
Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:

# 400

# 401

# 422
# Description: Prompt or Initial Image failed the safety checks.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Virtual Try-On with Ideogram 3.0
Examples.Description:
Performs virtual clothing try-on by automatically segmenting the clothing in the provided image
and replacing it based on the prompt. The user provides an image and an optional prompt describing
the desired clothing change (e.g., "change his shirt to a pink shirt").

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

Images links are available for a limited period of time; if you would like to keep the image, you must download it.
Examples.Code:
### Virtual Try-On with Ideogram 3.0
# @name post_try_on_v3
POST {{host}}/v1/ideogram-v3/try-on
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Try-on completed successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 401
# Description: Not authorized to generate an image.
# 422
# Description: Prompt or provided image failed safety check, or clothing segmentation failed.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.

Examples.Title: Add members to a specific organization
Examples.Description: Generated from OpenAPI examples.
Examples.Code:
### Add members to a specific organization
# @name add_organization_members
POST {{host}}/manage/api/organization/add_members?organization_id={{organization_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "members": [
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    },
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    }
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Results of adding members to the organization
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request
# 401
# Description: Not authorized
# 403
# Description: Not authorized - must be an organization owner
# 404
# Description: Organization not found

Examples.Description: Generated from OpenAPI examples.
Examples.Code:
### Retrieve user credit information and spending metrics
# @name get_user_credits
GET {{host}}/manage/api/credits?organization_id={{organization_id}}&start_time={{start_time}}&end_time={{end_time}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: User credit information retrieved successfully
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Not authorized
# 403
# Description: Not authorized

Examples.Title: Promote members to OWNER role in a specific organization
Examples.Description: Generated from OpenAPI examples.
Examples.Code:
### Promote members to OWNER role in a specific organization
# @name promote_organization_members
POST {{host}}/manage/api/organization/promote_members?organization_id={{organization_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "members": [
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    },
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    }
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Results of promoting members in the organization
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request
# 401
# Description: Not authorized
# 403
# Description: Not authorized - must be an organization owner
# 404
# Description: Organization not found

Examples.Title: Remove members from a specific organization
Examples.Description: Generated from OpenAPI examples.
Examples.Code:
### Remove members from a specific organization
# @name remove_organization_members
POST {{host}}/manage/api/organization/remove_members?organization_id={{organization_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "members": [
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    },
    {
      "display_handle": "john_doe",
      "email_address": "john.doe@example.com",
      "user_id": "dXNlcl8xMjM"
    }
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Results of removing members from the organization
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request
# 401
# Description: Not authorized
# 403
# Description: Not authorized - must be an organization owner
# 404
# Description: Organization not found

Examples.Title: Get model details
Examples.Description: Get detailed information about a specific custom model, including training run history for owned models. The user must either own the model or the model must be shared with the user's organization via the model registry. Returns 404 if the model is not found or not accessible.
Examples.Code:
### Get model details
# @name get_custom_model
GET {{host}}/models/{{model_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Model retrieved successfully
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized
# 404
# Description: Model not found

Examples.Title: List models
Examples.Description: Lists custom models for the authenticated user. Use the `scope` parameter to control which models are returned. `owned` (default) returns models created by the user. `shared` returns models shared with the user's organization via the model registry, excluding the user's own models.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Generate Magic Prompt
Examples.Description: Transforms basic prompts into a magic prompt. Internal use only (feature flagged). TODO: update description if enabled externally.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Ideogram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Describe
Examples.Description:
Describe an image.

Supported image formats include JPEG, PNG, and WebP.
Examples.Code:
### Describe
# @name post_describe
POST {{host}}/describe
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Description(s) created successfully.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid input provided.
# 422
# Description: Image failed the safety check.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests.
